talk_title: Acetylcholine signaling by T and B cells modulates tumor and liver regeneration

models.name: partial hepatectomy model
models.genotype_or_key_features: T cell-specific Chat knockout, B cell-specific Chat knockout

MOA_summary: T cell-derived acetylcholine promotes vascular dilation and immune cell trafficking, while B cell-derived acetylcholine is essential for liver regeneration after partial hepatectomy; loss of acetylcholine in T cells reduces tumor growth and alters immune phenotypes.
key_findings: B cell-specific deletion of Chat leads to lethality after 70% partial hepatectomy, indicating essential role in liver regeneration
T cell-specific Chat deletion reduces tumor growth and alters vascular density in mouse models
Serum choline levels correlate with patient prognosis across multiple cancer types
open_questions: How can acetylcholine signaling be targeted in an organ‑specific manner without affecting systemic cholinergic functions?

talk_title: Profiling and targeting innervating peripheral neurons in pancreatic cancer

models.name: SYNERGY trial
models.genotype_or_key_features: locally advanced PDAC, denervation + nab‑paclitaxel + PD‑1 inhibitor

MOA_summary: Fast‑Blue retrograde tracing identifies pancreas‑innervating sympathetic and sensory neurons; denervation with 6‑OHDA or nab‑paclitaxel reduces neuronal density, reprograms CAFs to a pro‑inflammatory state, enhances CD8 T‑cell infiltration, and synergizes with PD‑1 blockade to suppress tumor growth.
key_findings: Fast‑Blue tracing reveals identical numbers of neurons innervating normal pancreas and PDAC, but PDAC shows massive axonal sprouting
Denervation with 6‑OHDA (sympathetic) or nab‑paclitaxel (sensory) individually slows tumor growth; combined denervation nearly abolishes tumor progression in mouse models
Denervation converts immunosuppressive CAFs to pro‑inflammatory phenotype and increases CD8 T‑cell infiltration
Preclinical data support a phase I/II trial (SYNERGY) combining celiac ganglion radiotherapy, nab‑paclitaxel/gemcitabine, and PD‑1 inhibition
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Potential off‑target effects of 6‑OHDA on non‑neuronal catecholaminergic cells

combination_strategies.rationale: Chemotherapy induces peripheral neuropathy that reduces sensory innervation
combination_strategies.phenotype: synergistic tumor growth inhibition when combined with sympathetic denervation

combination_strategies.rationale: Denervation reprograms CAFs and restores T‑cell infiltration, sensitizing tumors to checkpoint blockade
combination_strategies.phenotype: enhanced anti‑tumor immunity and tumor regression in mouse PDAC

open_questions: What are the long‑term effects of celiac ganglion radiotherapy on autonomic function?
Can specific neuronal subpopulations be targeted without systemic neuropathy?

talk_title: Nociceptor neurons drive CD8 T‑cell exhaustion via CGRP in melanoma

targets.dependency_context: nociceptor‑derived CGRP acting on RAMP1+ CD8 T cells
CD8 T cells in melanoma microenvironment

biomarkers.name: RAMP1 expression
biomarkers.finding: High tumor RAMP1 correlates with poorer overall survival in melanoma patients

models.name: B16‑F10 melanoma model
models.genotype_or_key_features: TRPV1‑Cre DTR for nociceptor ablation; RAMP1‑KO CD8 T cells

models.name: melanoma patient cohort
models.genotype_or_key_features: TCGA and single‑cell datasets with RAMP1 expression

MOA_summary: Melanoma activates nociceptor neurons to release CGRP, which binds RAMP1 on CD8 T cells, driving an exhausted phenotype (↑PD‑1, LAG‑3, TIM‑3, ↓IFN‑γ); genetic or pharmacologic blockade of CGRP‑RAMP1 signaling restores T‑cell function and reduces tumor growth.
readouts: RNA‑seq of neurons and T cells
key_findings: Ablation of TRPV1+ nociceptors reduces melanoma growth and increases IFN‑γ+ CD8 T cells in tumors
Co‑culture of DRG neurons with melanoma up‑regulates CGRP expression in neurons
Exogenous CGRP induces PD‑1, LAG‑3, TIM‑3 up‑regulation and IFN‑γ loss in CD8 T cells; effect absent in RAMP1‑KO T cells
RAMP1‑deficient CD8 T cells show reduced exhaustion and slower tumor growth when transferred into melanoma‑bearing mice
High RAMP1 expression in melanoma patients associates with worse survival and reduced response to checkpoint blockade
Retrospective analysis of migraine patients on CGRP antagonists suggests improved outcomes in melanoma with lung metastasis
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: CGRP signaling appears selective for a subset of Nav1.8+ nociceptors (~25% CGRP+)
Potential off‑target effects of systemic CGRP blockade on vascular tone

combination_strategies.rationale: Denervation or CGRP blockade restores T‑cell function, potentially synergizing with checkpoint blockade
combination_strategies.phenotype: enhanced tumor regression in mouse melanoma

combination_strategies.rationale: Pharmacologic inhibition of CGRP‑RAMP1 axis to prevent T‑cell exhaustion
combination_strategies.phenotype: reduced tumor growth and improved survival in mouse models

open_questions: What upstream tumor factors induce CGRP secretion from nociceptors?
Can CGRP antagonists be safely combined with existing immunotherapies in patients?
